app.name: ATO
app.tagline: Agentic Tool Optimization — the control panel for AI coding tools

home.heroTitle: Your local war room for humans and LLMs.
home.heroSubtitle: Decide together, call real tools, walk out with a signed audit trail. Drop in any of your LLMs — Claude, GPT, Gemini, Grok, MiniMax, and 15+ more. Push back on them. Cite the repo. Ship the decision.
home.heroUseCases: Strategy debates · Pre-mortems · Architecture decisions · Code review · Security audits
home.quickHint: Persona on top of any runtime — @security-specialist on Gemini, @perf-reviewer on MiniMax, etc. Drop them into a war room session.
home.noAgentsBody: Create your first agent to see it here.
home.noRunsBody: Run an agent and the most recent executions show here.
home.connectRuntimeTitle: Connect a runtime to get started
home.connectRuntimeBody: We didn't detect Claude, Codex, Gemini, OpenClaw, or Hermes on your machine. Open Settings → Runtimes to connect one (or use a stored API key).

guided.askGoal: Hi! What do you want your agent to help with? Describe it in plain English.
guided.goalPlaceholder: e.g., review my pull requests for security issues
guided.thinking: Thinking about the right runtime, model, skills, and MCPs…
guided.doneBody: {{name}} was created. Open it from the Agents list.
guided.noRuntimeBody: The chat wizard uses your existing CLI subscription (Claude, Codex, Gemini, Hermes, OpenClaw) to suggest a stack. None were detected. Open Settings → Runtimes to connect one, or use the Quick (form) path which doesn't need a runtime to be live.
guided.willConnectHint: After confirming, ATO installs these MCP servers and wires them to your agent.
guided.doneWithMcps: {{name}} was created. Now connect the MCP tools below so it can actually do its job.
guided.connectMcpsHint: Your agent needs these MCP servers to actually do its job. Click Install to add them to {{runtime}}.

quick.name: Name
quick.descriptionPlaceholder: One-line description of what this agent does
quick.systemPromptPlaceholder: You are a code reviewer focused on security…
quick.noSkillsAvailable: No skills installed for this runtime.
quick.successBody: {{name}} is ready. Open it from the Agents list.

agents.newSubtitle: Two paths: a chat that suggests a stack, or a one-page form for power users.

myAgents.emptyBody: Use "+ New" to create your first agent. Built-in runtime agents (Claude Code subagents, OpenClaw gateway agents) are listed under the Built-in tab.

variables.title: Variables
variables.subtitle: Use {{'{'}}variableName{{'}'}} in your prompts. ATO resolves each variable at dispatch time so the agent always sees fresh, contextual data — not a static blob.
variables.name: Name
variables.emptyBody: Add a variable like {{'{'}}user_name{{'}'}} or {{'{'}}project_path{{'}'}}, and reference it in your system prompt.
variables.unresolvedHint: Add a resolver below or remove the token from the prompt.

fields.projectPathHint: No config — resolves to whichever project is active when the agent runs.
fields.proStub: This resolver is wired in Wave 2.2. Storing the config now is fine; runtime resolution will activate when the implementation lands.

context.placeholder: Pre-call context hooks land in Wave 2.2. The schema (agent_hooks) is already in place; Pro users will configure hooks here that fetch CRM/file/MCP data and inject it into every dispatch.

memory.placeholder: Conversation memory policy lands in Wave 2.2. The schema (memory_policy_json) is already on the agents table; Free users will get fixed defaults, Pro users get tunable threshold + summarizer model.

models.placeholder: Per-task model selection lands in Wave 2.2. router/summarizer/response/evaluator slots will let you use cheap fast models for routing and advanced models only for the final response.

terminal.shellWebOnly: Shell mode requires the desktop app.

mcpManager.addSubtitle: Browse the registry or paste a custom command. ATO will give you the exact config snippet to drop into your runtime.
mcpManager.noResults: No MCP servers match your search.
mcpManager.installHint: Paste this into the mcpServers section of your runtime config (.claude/mcp.json, .gemini/settings.json, etc.) and restart the runtime.
mcpManager.name: Name

runtimes.authSubtitle: Use your existing CLI subscription (like VS Code rides your GitHub login) or a stored API key. Both can be configured simultaneously — pick which one is active per runtime.
runtimes.cliNotFound: {{cmd}} not detected. Install + log in to use this method.

auth.name: Name
auth.passwordRequirements: Min 8 characters, uppercase, lowercase, and number

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

context.title: Context Usage
context.subtitle: Real-time breakdown of your context window
context.depInfo: All sources loaded into the current context window, grouped by type.

context.fileNotFound: File not available in this session

prompt.placeholder: Ask via MCP — context, skills, usage, servers... (simulated)
prompt.newQuickChatTitle: One-on-one chat in this pane. Picks the runtime/agent currently selected; can hop runtimes per message.
prompt.newSessionTitle: Multi-turn session with lifecycle (open / close / coordinator summary). Opens in the Sessions tab.
prompt.newWarRoomTitle: Fire the same prompt to every connected LLM in parallel. Opens the First-Chat Wizard.

skills.title: Skills Manager
skills.subtitle: Manage your Claude Code skills
skills.noSkillsHint: Scan your filesystem or create a new skill
skills.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this skill?
skills.name: Skill Name

detail.triggerHint: Claude uses this description to decide when to auto-load this skill. Use clear, keyword-rich language matching how users naturally ask.
detail.overLimitHint: Anthropic recommends keeping SKILL.md under 500 lines. Move detailed reference material to separate support files (reference.md, examples.md) and link them from the main skill.
detail.linkedFilesHint: Files referenced from skill content. Claude loads these on-demand using relative paths.

conflicts.title: Description Overlap Detected
conflicts.hint: Skills with similar descriptions may cause Claude to auto-invoke the wrong one. Since skills don't require /commands, clear differentiation in descriptions is critical.

create.directoryHint: Creates a folder with scripts/, references/, and assets/ subdirectories
create.aiGenerateHint: Describe what you want the skill to do and AI will write the content for you.
create.aiPromptPlaceholder: e.g., A skill that enforces our API naming conventions and validates REST endpoint patterns...

skills.dragHint: Drag to reorder priority within scope

pasteJson.title: Paste workflow JSON
pasteJson.description: Paste a workflow with "nodes" and "edges" arrays. This replaces the current canvas.
pasteJson.errorJson: Could not parse JSON — check the syntax.
pasteJson.errorShape: Workflow must be an object with "nodes" and "edges" arrays.
pasteJson.errorNode: Each node needs a non-empty string "id".
pasteJson.errorEdge: Each edge needs string "from" and "to" fields.

projects.memorySubtitle: CLAUDE.md files Claude reads on start — hierarchy: user → project → nested
projects.hooksEmpty: No hooks configured. Hooks run shell commands on events like pre-tool-use or post-commit.
projects.permissionsSubtitle: allow / deny / ask rules from settings.json
projects.mcpEmpty: No MCP servers configured. MCP servers extend your agent with external tools.
projects.sandboxSubtitle: OpenAI Agents SDK execution sandbox configuration
projects.sandboxEmpty: No sandbox configuration found. Sandbox isolates agent execution in Docker containers with network + filesystem policies.
projects.policiesSubtitle: Per-tool approval rules for OpenAI Agents SDK
projects.policiesEmpty: No approval policies configured. Policies control which tools agents can use without asking.
projects.settingsFilesSubtitle: Source of truth for hooks, permissions, and MCP
projects.recentEditsSubtitle: Last 15 file writes (this project + inherited globals)
projects.recentEditsEmpty: No edits yet. Changes you save will appear here.
projects.tokenBreakdownSubtitle: Estimated tokens this project's config would consume when loaded
projects.tokenBreakdownEmpty: No measurable tokens yet. Add some CLAUDE.md content and skills to see breakdown.

ollama.subtitle: Local model server — OpenAI-compatible endpoint for any runtime

ollama.startToSee: Start Ollama to see available models.

editor.noBackups: No backups yet. Each save automatically creates one in ~/.ato/backups/.
editor.fileChangedDesc: Someone (or another process) wrote to this file after you opened it. Reload to see the latest version, or overwrite to force your changes.
editor.globalConfigWarning: I understand this edits a global config that affects all projects using this runtime.

subtitles.intro: ATO — the GUI for daily agentic work.
subtitles.oneWorkspace: One workspace for Claude, Codex, Gemini, OpenClaw, Hermes, Ollama.
subtitles.threeWays: Three ways to create an agent. Let's see all of them.
subtitles.oneClickSave: One click to save — file written, agent registered, ready to run.
subtitles.bundleGroup: Bundle them into a group. Router decides who handles what.
subtitles.twoGroupTypes: Two group types: routed (router picks one) and automation (pipeline).
subtitles.automationPipeline: Now the automation pipeline — one prompt, the whole workflow runs.
subtitles.swapMidThread: Now swap back to Claude mid-thread — the full history travels.
subtitles.scheduleUnattended: And you can schedule it to run unattended.
subtitles.wakeFromSleep: Wake-from-sleep on macOS, Linux, and Windows — jobs fire even when ATO is closed.
subtitles.closeBuildBundle: Build the agents. Bundle them into a workflow. Run on demand or on a schedule.

providerKeys.title: Provider Keys
providerKeys.intro: Register API keys for the providers you use. The cloud poller fetches your daily usage so the Usage tab shows authoritative totals — including activity from phone apps and web UIs the local watcher can't see. Keys are encrypted at rest under AES-256-GCM with per-user AAD; the plaintext is sent only on registration and never returned.
providerKeys.empty: No provider keys yet. Add one above to start tracking cross-device usage.
providerKeys.keyPlaceholder: Paste the key — it's encrypted server-side and never echoed back.
providerKeys.confirmRevoke: Revoke this key? The cron will stop polling it, but its audit row stays for forensics.

providerNote.viable: Daily cron polls this provider's usage API and writes authoritative totals to your dashboard.
providerNote.balanceOnly: {{provider}} only exposes a balance endpoint, not historical aggregates. The key is stored for when the upcoming client-side capture path (PR-D, Pro feature) lands. Your dashboard will show no rows for this provider until then.
providerNote.noAggregate: {{provider}} doesn't expose an aggregate usage endpoint to third parties. The key is stored for when the upcoming client-side capture path (PR-D, Pro feature) lands. Your dashboard will show no rows for this provider until then.

missions.title: Missions
missions.subtitle: Local mission-control board. Dispatch and tick run CLI-side.
missions.loadError: Failed to load missions — is the ATO DB available?
missions.confirmComplete: Mark this mission as complete? Worktree cleanup runs CLI-side via `ato missions cleanup`.
